task_description: Screen each abstract for a systematic review on ML methods for predicting maternal or neonatal adverse outcomes during pregnancy or childbirth. INCLUDE a paper only if it presents or evaluates an ML model that predicts a complication of pregnancy, labour, or the neonatal period (e.g., preeclampsia, preterm birth, fetal distress, neonatal sepsis, stillbirth). Papers about postpartum outcomes beyond 28 days, fertility, gynaecological cancer, or reproductive genetics should be EXCLUDE.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for Preeclampsia Risk Prediction in the First Trimester from Maternal Biomarkers
papers.abstract: Early identification of preeclampsia risk enables preventive low-dose aspirin therapy. We train a gradient boosting model on 12,000 singleton pregnancies using first-trimester blood pressure, uterine artery Doppler, PAPP-A, PlGF, and maternal history. AUC 0.91 for severe preeclampsia before 34 weeks is achieved, outperforming the FMF combined algorithm and logistic regression. External validation on two independent cohorts confirms generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, preeclampsia, first trimester, biomarkers, Doppler
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, 12k pregnancies, AUC 0.91, FMF algorithm comparison, two external validations
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for first-trimester preeclampsia prediction AUC 0.91, outperforming FMF algorithm.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Automated Fetal Biometry Measurement from Ultrasound Scans
papers.abstract: Accurate fetal biometry supports gestational age estimation and growth monitoring. We train a U-Net-based system on 48,000 obstetric ultrasound frames to automate measurement of biparietal diameter, head circumference, abdominal circumference, and femur length. Mean absolute error 1.8 mm is achieved. This is a measurement automation tool; it does not predict pregnancy complications or adverse outcomes.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, fetal biometry, ultrasound, measurement automation
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, 48k ultrasound frames, MAE 1.8 mm — measurement not outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net for automated fetal biometry, MAE 1.8 mm, not outcome prediction.

papers.title: LSTM for Intrapartum Fetal Hypoxia Detection from Cardiotocography
papers.abstract: Fetal hypoxia during labour is detectable from CTG patterns but CTG interpretation has poor inter-observer agreement. We train an LSTM on 1,800 CTG recordings from the CTU-UHB dataset to predict intrapartum fetal acidosis within 30 minutes of delivery. Sensitivity 0.81 at specificity 0.82 is achieved, substantially outperforming visual FIGO criteria and a frequency-domain SVM baseline.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, CTG, cardiotocography, fetal hypoxia, intrapartum
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, CTU-UHB 1800 CTGs, sensitivity 0.81, FIGO criteria and SVM baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for intrapartum fetal acidosis prediction from CTG, sensitivity 0.81, outperforming FIGO criteria.

papers.title: ML for Predicting Endometriosis Severity and Infertility Treatment Response
papers.abstract: Endometriosis affects fertility and its severity predicts IVF success. We train a support vector machine on laparoscopic findings, AMH levels, and patient history from 800 endometriosis patients to predict IVF treatment outcome. AUC 0.74 is achieved. This work concerns infertility treatment and reproductive outcomes beyond the 28-day neonatal period.
papers.topic_hint: SVM, endometriosis, IVF, infertility, treatment response
papers.methodology_hint: SVM, IVF outcome, 800 endometriosis patients — infertility treatment not pregnancy complication
papers.claimed_contribution: SVM for IVF outcome prediction in endometriosis, AUC 0.74, infertility not pregnancy complication.

papers.title: Random Forest for Predicting Spontaneous Preterm Birth from Cervical Length and Biomarkers
papers.abstract: Preterm birth affects 10% of pregnancies globally. We train a random forest on second-trimester cervical length, fetal fibronectin, progesterone levels, and prior obstetric history from 4,600 singleton pregnancies to predict spontaneous preterm birth before 34 weeks. AUC 0.81 is achieved. Comparison against cervical length threshold alone, fetal fibronectin alone, and a logistic regression baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, preterm birth, cervical length, fetal fibronectin, second trimester
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, 4600 pregnancies, AUC 0.81, 3 comparison methods
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest for preterm birth prediction AUC 0.81, 3 clinical and statistical comparisons.

papers.title: CNN for Automated Ovarian Follicle Counting in Fertility Ultrasound
papers.abstract: Antral follicle count guides ovarian stimulation protocols in IVF. We develop a CNN to automate follicle counting from transvaginal ultrasound. Mean absolute error 1.9 follicles per scan is achieved. This work supports fertility treatment planning; it does not predict pregnancy or neonatal outcomes.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, follicle counting, ultrasound, IVF, fertility
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, follicle counting, fertility ultrasound — IVF planning not pregnancy outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for automated follicle counting, MAE 1.9, fertility planning not pregnancy outcome.

papers.title: Transformer for Predicting Neonatal Hyperbilirubinaemia Requiring Phototherapy
papers.abstract: Neonatal jaundice requiring phototherapy is common and potentially serious. We train a transformer on neonatal EHR features at birth — gestational age, birth weight, blood group, feeding type, and first bilirubin — from 28,000 neonates to predict phototherapy need within 72 hours. AUROC 0.88 is achieved, outperforming the BiliTool nomogram and a logistic regression baseline.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, neonatal jaundice, hyperbilirubinaemia, phototherapy prediction
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 28k neonates, AUROC 0.88, BiliTool and LR baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for neonatal hyperbilirubinaemia prediction AUROC 0.88, outperforming BiliTool.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Placental Gene Expression Patterns in Adverse Pregnancy Outcomes
papers.abstract: Placental transcriptomics carries information about pregnancy complications. We construct gene co-expression networks from placental RNA-seq of 320 term and preterm deliveries and apply a GNN to classify delivery type. AUC 0.80 is achieved. This is a transcriptomic classification study; the model requires post-delivery placental tissue and cannot predict outcomes prospectively during pregnancy.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, placental transcriptomics, RNA-seq, pregnancy outcomes, gene expression
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, placental RNA-seq, AUC 0.80 — post-delivery tissue not prospective outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for classifying delivery type from placental transcriptomics, AUC 0.80, post-delivery analysis.

papers.title: XGBoost for Predicting Emergency Caesarean Section During Labour Using Intrapartum EHR
papers.abstract: Unplanned caesarean section is associated with maternal and neonatal morbidity. We train XGBoost on intrapartum EHR features — labour progress, CTG features, maternal demographics, and analgesia use — from 35,000 labours to predict emergency caesarean section. AUROC 0.79 is achieved. An external validation on a different hospital system confirms generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, caesarean section, intrapartum EHR, labour outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost for emergency caesarean prediction AUROC 0.79, external validation.

papers.title: Precision Medicine for Cervical Cancer Staging Using Deep Learning on MRI
papers.abstract: Accurate cervical cancer staging guides treatment planning. We train a 3D convolutional network on pelvic MRI from 480 cervical cancer patients to classify FIGO stage. Accuracy 0.84 is achieved. This is a gynaecological cancer classification task; it does not predict maternal or neonatal outcomes during pregnancy.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, cervical cancer, MRI, FIGO staging, gynaecological oncology
papers.methodology_hint: 3D CNN, pelvic MRI, cervical cancer staging — gynaecological cancer not pregnancy outcomes
papers.claimed_contribution: 3D CNN for cervical cancer FIGO staging, accuracy 0.84, gynaecological oncology not pregnancy.

papers.title: LSTM for Early Detection of Neonatal Sepsis from Continuous Monitoring Streams
papers.abstract: Neonatal sepsis is a leading cause of neonatal mortality. We train an LSTM on continuous heart rate variability, respiratory rate, and SpO2 monitoring streams from 1,400 neonatal ICU admissions to detect sepsis onset 8 hours before clinical recognition. AUROC 0.87 is achieved, outperforming the NEOMOD score and a single-vital-sign threshold baseline.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, neonatal sepsis, NICU, monitoring streams, early detection
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, 1400 NICU admissions, AUROC 0.87, NEOMOD and threshold baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for neonatal sepsis early detection AUROC 0.87, NICU monitoring streams.

papers.title: Bayesian Network for Modelling Risk Factors of Stillbirth in Low-Income Countries
papers.abstract: Stillbirth rates in low-income countries are disproportionately high. We apply a Bayesian network to population-level obstetric data from 82,000 pregnancies in three sub-Saharan African cohorts to identify modifiable risk factors for stillbirth. The model identifies antenatal care access, malaria, and anaemia as primary causal risk factors. Prospective stillbirth risk prediction during ongoing pregnancy is not performed; this is a causal risk factor analysis.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian network, stillbirth, risk factors, sub-Saharan Africa, causal analysis
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian network, 82k pregnancies, causal risk factors — risk factor analysis not prospective prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian network for stillbirth risk factor identification, causal analysis not prediction.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Predicting Gestational Hypertension Onset from Routine Antenatal Data
papers.abstract: Gestational hypertension is a precursor to preeclampsia affecting 5% of pregnancies. We train a deep neural network on routine antenatal visit data — blood pressure trajectories, urine protein dipstick, weight gain, and oedema — from 18,000 pregnancies to predict gestational hypertension onset before 36 weeks. AUROC 0.83 is achieved. Only logistic regression is compared.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, gestational hypertension, antenatal data, blood pressure, pregnancy
papers.methodology_hint: DNN, 18k pregnancies, AUROC 0.83, gestational hypertension — only LR comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: DNN for gestational hypertension onset prediction AUROC 0.83, single logistic regression comparison.

papers.title: Ensemble ML for Postpartum Haemorrhage Risk Prediction at Time of Delivery
papers.abstract: Postpartum haemorrhage is the leading cause of maternal mortality worldwide. We train an ensemble of XGBoost and logistic regression on intrapartum EHR features from 62,000 deliveries to predict PPH requiring transfusion. AUROC 0.81 is achieved with external validation on a different country's maternity database. PPH occurs within 24 hours of delivery, within the peripartum period.
papers.topic_hint: ensemble ML, postpartum haemorrhage, maternal mortality, delivery risk
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost + LR ensemble, 62k deliveries, AUROC 0.81, external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Ensemble ML for PPH prediction AUROC 0.81, external validation.

papers.title: Genomic Prediction of Polygenic Risk Scores for Gestational Diabetes in South Asian Women
papers.abstract: South Asian ancestry confers elevated gestational diabetes risk. We compute polygenic risk scores from SNP arrays in 3,800 South Asian women and combine with clinical variables in a logistic regression to predict gestational diabetes. This is a genomic prediction study; no real-time antenatal monitoring model or clinical risk tool is developed for labour or neonatal outcomes.
papers.topic_hint: polygenic risk score, gestational diabetes, SNP array, South Asian, genomics
papers.methodology_hint: PRS + LR, SNP arrays, gestational diabetes — genomic prediction not labour or neonatal outcome
papers.claimed_contribution: PRS for gestational diabetes prediction in South Asian women, genomic not clinical monitoring.
